word.definition: Lacuna is a formal word that refers to a gap or blank space in something—in other words, a missing part. When used with respect to biology, lacuna also refers to a small cavity, pit, or discontinuity in an anatomical structure.

sports_fact.text: In 1982, Joel Youngblood got hits for two different teams in two different cities on the same day — traded mid-day from the Mets, he flew to Philadelphia and singled for the Expos that night.

market.headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Closes at Record High Amid Hopes of Mideast Progress
market.summary: Oil turns lower; Nvidia slips despite strong results

quote.text: In a free society a large degree of human activity is none of the government's business. We should make criminal what's going to hurt other people and other than that we should leave it to people to make their own choices.
quote.author: Barney Frank

history.text: American Civil War: The Union Army succeeds in closing off the last escape route from Port Hudson, Louisiana, in preparation for the coming siege.
